message:
perf(indexer): versioned lexical rebuild packet pipeline with parallel page-prep workers + runtime budget tracking

This is a large-scope reshape of the grouped lexical rebuild pipeline
that replaces the ad-hoc `PendingLexicalRebuildConversation` flow with
a versioned packet protocol, explicit producer / page-prep /
consumer workers, and an end-to-end runtime budget snapshot that is
now surfaced in `state.json` via `state_meta_json`. The aim is to
keep Tantivy rebuilds saturating multiple cores without blowing the
bounded in-flight message budget and, equally importantly, to give
operators a real-time view into the pipeline's internal state so we
can diagnose stalls without re-instrumenting after the fact.

================================================================
1) LexicalRebuildConversationPacket
================================================================

`src/indexer/mod.rs`

  - New `LexicalRebuildConversationPacket` struct with
    `LexicalRebuildPacketDiagnostics { version, source,
    provenance_mode, missing_conversation_id }` embedded. The
    diagnostics are populated at packet-construction time so every
    downstream consumer (ingester, flow-reservation accountant,
    runtime snapshotter) can reason about provenance without
    re-deriving it.
  - `LEXICAL_REBUILD_PACKET_VERSION = 1`. The version field is
    deliberately on the diagnostics payload so we can evolve the
    packet shape in a wire-format-friendly way as additional
    rebuild sources land.
  - `LexicalRebuildPacketSource` ∈ { `CanonicalReplay`,
    `NormalizedConversation` } distinguishes packets that come from
    the on-disk canonical store from ones synthesized off a
    freshly-normalized conversation without a DB round-trip.
  - `LexicalRebuildPacketProvenanceMode` ∈ { `SourceMapLookup`,
    `ConversationFields`, `MetadataFields`, `HostFallback`,
    `LocalDefault` } records how the `(source_id, origin_host)`
    pair was actually resolved. This was previously implicit in
    a cascade of `or_else` calls and made it impossible to tell
    from logs which branch had fired for a given packet.

Two new conversion helpers:
  - `lexical_rebuild_packet_provenance_from_canonical(...)` takes
    raw canonical-store fields (source_id, origin_host, path,
    metadata) and returns a normalized tuple plus the
    provenance_mode enum.
  - `lexical_rebuild_packet_provenance_from_metadata(...)` parses
    metadata-JSON-backed sources for normalized conversations.

`prepare_lexical_rebuild_packet_from_canonical` and
`prepare_lexical_rebuild_packet_batch` assemble packets (including
the grouped message rows) and batch them up for the page-prep
worker pool.

================================================================
2) Parallel page-prep worker pool
================================================================

`src/indexer/mod.rs`

The old code serialized page preparation on the same thread that
read from the DB. That throttled rebuilds on large workspaces
because page prep (field assembly, tokenization precompute,
synonym expansion) was genuinely CPU-bound.

New worker-pool architecture:

  - `spawn_lexical_rebuild_packet_producer(...)` reads packets
    from the DB stream and feeds a bounded channel.
  - `spawn_lexical_rebuild_page_prep_workers(...)` spins up N page-
    prep workers where N is derived from available parallelism
    minus reserved cores (see "runtime knobs" below).
  - Prepared pages are released via
    `release_lexical_rebuild_prepared_page_reservation` /
    `release_completed_lexical_rebuild_pages` so the in-flight
    byte budget is bounded and pages are returned to the pool
    promptly after the ingester flushes.
  - `prepare_lexical_rebuild_page_work(...)` is the actual worker
    body; it pulls from the packet channel, computes the page
    reservation bytes, assembles the prepared page, and hands it
    off to the ordered buffer.

`ingest_batch` and `rebuild_tantivy_from_db_with_options` are
rewritten (still present in the diff but reshaped) to drive the
worker pool instead of doing the work inline.

================================================================
3) Runtime knobs (all deterministic, no env overrides by default)
================================================================

`src/indexer/mod.rs`

Each of these has a pure helper so they can be unit-tested without
touching the full pipeline:

  - `lexical_rebuild_available_parallelism()` – std parallelism
    with a conservative floor.
  - `lexical_rebuild_default_reserved_cores_for_available(...)` –
    how many cores we hold back for non-rebuild work (ingester,
    watcher, connector scans).
  - `lexical_rebuild_reserved_cores_for_available(...)` – honors
    a configured override if one was set (via the new pipeline
    settings snapshot) but clamps to a safe range.
  - `lexical_rebuild_worker_parallelism_for_available_and_reserved(...)`
  - `lexical_rebuild_default_worker_parallelism_for_available(...)`
  - `lexical_rebuild_configured_worker_parallelism_for_available(...)`
  - `lexical_rebuild_pipeline_channel_size()` – chosen to keep
    backpressure from starving workers without unbounded memory.
  - `lexical_rebuild_page_prep_worker_parallelism(pipeline_channel_size)`
  - `lexical_rebuild_first_budget_promotion_wait()` – how long we
    wait before promoting the initial conservative budget to the
    steady-state budget.
  - `lexical_rebuild_pipeline_max_message_bytes_in_flight(...)` –
    the hard byte cap that gates packet admission.

================================================================
4) Runtime snapshot exposed in state.json
================================================================

`src/indexer/mod.rs` + `src/lib.rs`

  - `capture_lexical_rebuild_pipeline_runtime(...)` and
    `refresh_lexical_rebuild_pipeline_runtime(...)` maintain a
    crash-safe on-disk snapshot of `{ queue_depth,
    inflight_message_bytes, pending_batch_conversations,
    pending_batch_message_bytes, page_prep_workers,
    active_page_prep_jobs, ordered_buffered_pages,
    budget_generation, updated_at_ms }`.
  - `state_meta_json` in `src/lib.rs` now loads the active snapshot
    when `lexical.rebuilding` is true and embeds the full
    `lexical_rebuild_pipeline_settings_snapshot()` under
    `.lexical.pipeline`, including a `.runtime` sub-object with
    RFC3339-formatted `updated_at`. The non-rebuild case falls
    back to `runtime: null` so schema consumers can rely on the
    key always existing.

This was the main motivating use-case: `cass state` now shows you
the queue depth and in-flight bytes in real time, and the
`pipeline.runtime.updated_at` field lets watchers detect a wedged
pipeline from a stale snapshot.

================================================================
5) Storage: normalized (source_id, origin_host) at read time
================================================================

`src/storage/sqlite.rs`

`FrankenStorage`'s conversation-reading paths and the lexical-
rebuild conversation row reader used to return
`source_id.unwrap_or_else(|| "local".to_string())` and the raw
`origin_host` column, leaving downstream normalization to each
caller. That diverged from the packet's provenance model and made
it possible for rebuild packets to disagree with the canonical DB
view on identical rows.

Both readers now route the raw columns through
`normalized_storage_source_parts(raw_source_id, None, raw_origin_host)`
which applies the same normalization the packet pipeline uses,
so rows read via `FrankenStorage::get_conversations` and rows
read via the lexical rebuild stream always agree on
`(source_id, origin_host)`.

Also:
  - `LexicalRebuildGroupedMessageRow` now derives `PartialEq, Eq`
    so the new unit tests can compare grouped message vectors
    directly instead of going through a JSON roundtrip.

================================================================
6) Tests
================================================================

`src/lib.rs`

`cli_read_db_tests` picks up `use serial_test::serial;` and +194
lines of new test coverage for the `state_meta_json` pipeline
embedding (rebuild-active vs rebuild-idle shapes, runtime
snapshot missing-file fallback, and round-trip of the
RFC3339-formatted `updated_at`).

================================================================
7) Beads tracking
================================================================

`.beads/issues.jsonl` + `.beads/last-touched` pick up the 19 new
tracking entries related to this work and the in-flight benchmarks
tracked around it (packet version evolution, page-prep worker
tuning, pipeline runtime schema). No beads were closed-then-reopened
and no existing issue text was deleted.

Net diff: +5525 / -2129 across five files, all additive or in-
place refactors; no functions removed without a replacement (the
"removed" `fn` list in the diff – `should_commit_lexical_rebuild`,
`should_persist_lexical_rebuild_progress`,
`write_json_pretty_atomically`, `rebuild_tantivy_from_db_with_options`,
`ingest_batch`, `build_watch_roots`, `watch_sources`,
`reset_storage`, `reindex_paths`, etc. – are all in-place
signature/body changes that the diff printer counted as
delete+re-add).

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
